short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $7.73B in FY2026 Q1, +1.3% YoY — decelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $13.10B (+69.5% vs current Q) — set by 35 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $0.10.
Next earnings: 2026-07-23 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is mixed — execution risk on the next guide is elevated.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $32B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $54B → FY2 $59B (+9.3% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 28.0% — structural premium business over the long-cycle view.
7-quarter run-rate CAGR: -28.7% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Information Technology): Multi-year AI/cloud capex cycle remains the dominant secular theme.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 110.9% — extreme (binary-event hedging implied).
Put/Call OI: 1.53 — put-heavy hedging (institutions defensive).
30-day implied move: ±19.6% ($85.85 – $127.69) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -1.18pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $70.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Sell 30–45 DTE cash-secured put at $101 (5% OTM). High IV (111%) + uptrend → collect premium with a willingness to be assigned at a discount. Roll if breached, otherwise let theta decay.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 78.7% across 9 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 9 dates spanning 98–616 DTE.
90-day implied move: ±43.3% ($60.58 – $152.96).
217-day implied move: ±60.4% ($42.24 – $171.30) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $24 (-77.5% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-32.3pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $96 put / short $117 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
